description: Lock Industrial Design Profession to source-backed mass-produced product design around Behrens/AEG rather than generic factory, trade-catalog, or museum-catalog prerequisites.

checks.reason: Britannica anchors Behrens's AEG artistic-adviser role and industrial-design lineage to 1907.
Industrial design is defined around mass-produced consumer products and manufacturable goods.
Factory organization is too broad and indirect for the corrected professional product-design scope.
Catalogs are possible industrial-design outputs, not a direct prerequisite.
Museum collection catalogs are outside the direct industrial-design profession scope.
